title: 224. Basic Calculator
question:
Implement a basic calculator to evaluate a simple expression string.

The expression string may contain open ( and closing parentheses ), the plus + or minus sign -, non-negative integers and empty spaces .

Example 1:

Input: "1 + 1"
Output: 2

Example 2:

Input: " 2-1 + 2 "
Output: 3

Example 3:

Input: "(1+(4+5+2)-3)+(6+8)"
Output: 23
Note:
  • You may assume that the given expression is always valid.
  • Do not use the eval built-in library function.
code: class Solution(object):\u000A    def calculate(self, s):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        :type s: str\u000A        :rtype: int\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        s \u003D list((\u0027((%s)\u0027%s).replace(\u0027 \u0027, \u0027\u0027))\u000A        nested_brackets, num \u003D [], []\u000A        for i, c in enumerate(s):\u000A            if c \u003D\u003D \u0027(\u0027:\u000A                nested_brackets.append([0, \u0027+\u0027])\u000A                num \u003D []\u000A            elif c \u003D\u003D \u0027\u002D\u0027 and s[i\u002D1] in \u0027+\u002D(\u0027:\u000A                prev_sign \u003D nested_brackets[\u002D1][1]\u000A                nested_brackets[\u002D1][1] \u003D \u0027+\u0027 if prev_sign \u003D\u003D \u0027\u002D\u0027 else \u0027+\u0027\u000A            elif c in \u0027+\u002D)\u0027:\u000A                if num:\u000A                    num \u003D int(\u0027\u0027.join(num))\u000A                    sign \u003D (1 if nested_brackets[\u002D1][1] \u003D\u003D \u0027+\u0027 else \u002D1)\u000A                    nested_brackets[\u002D1][0] +\u003D sign * num\u000A                    num \u003D []\u000A                    if c \u003D\u003D \u0027)\u0027:\u000A                        num \u003D list(str(nested_brackets[\u002D1][0]))\u000A                        nested_brackets.pop()\u000A                    else: nested_brackets[\u002D1][1] \u003D c\u000A            elif c.isdigit(): num.append(c)\u000A        return int(\u0027\u0027.join(num))
